---
title: "FIT5125 - Assignment 2"
author: "Bharath Arun Gandhimani"
date: "2025-11-06"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown


```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(readxl)

file_path <- "Assignment_2_Task_A_Database.xlsx"
room_hops <- read_excel(file_path, sheet = "room_hops")
room_duration <- read_excel(file_path, sheet = "room_duration")

room_hops <- room_hops %>% mutate(condition = trimws(condition))
room_duration <- room_duration %>% mutate(condition = trimws(condition))

avg_room_hops <- room_hops %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  summarise(avg_room_hops = mean(room_hops, na.rm = TRUE))

session_durations <- room_duration %>%
  group_by(participant_code, condition) %>%
  summarise(session_duration = sum(room_duration_minutes, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

avg_session_duration <- session_durations %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  summarise(avg_session_duration = mean(session_duration, na.rm = TRUE))

metrics <- left_join(avg_room_hops, avg_session_duration, by = "condition") %>%
  filter(condition != "Cancelled") # remove 'Cancelled' if unwanted

metrics_long <- metrics %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c("avg_room_hops", "avg_session_duration"),
               names_to = "metric",
               values_to = "value")

# Plot with log scale and value labels
ggplot(metrics_long, aes(x = condition, y = value, fill = metric)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(value, 2)), 
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
            vjust = -0.25, size = 3.5) +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = "log10") +
  labs(title = "Participant Engagement Metrics by Experimental Condition",
       x = "Experimental Condition",
       y = "Metric Value (log scale)",
       fill = "Metric") +
  scale_fill_manual(labels = c("Average Room Hops", "Average Session Duration (min)"),
                    values = c("steelblue", "darkorange")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1))


```
```{r}
# Load required packages
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(MASS)
library(ggfortify)

# 1. Load data from both sheets (update path as needed)
survey <- read_excel("Assignment_2_Task_B_Database.xlsx", sheet = "Survey_Data")
summary <- read_excel("Assignment_2_Task_B_Database.xlsx", sheet = "Outgoing_Summary")

# 2. Merge and clean columns
df <- merge(
  survey,
  summary,
  by.x = "Participant_Code",
  by.y = "From_Participant"
)
df$Age_Range <- as.factor(df[["Age Range"]])
df$First_Language <- as.factor(df[["First Language"]])
df <- df[, c("Participant_Code", "Expressiveness_Avg", "Age_Range", "First_Language", "Unique_People_Spoke_To")]

# 3. Set professional margin theme for all plots
margin_theme <- theme(
  plot.margin = margin(t = 25, r = 20, b = 35, l = 20),
  plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15, hjust = 0.5),
  plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
  axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")
)

# Helper for rotating x-axis labels
angled_labels <- theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# 4. Make binned histograms with readable x axis & bar value labels

# (A) Histogram for Unique People Spoken To (bin size 3)
hist_data <- df %>%
  mutate(bin = cut(Unique_People_Spoke_To, breaks = seq(0, max(df$Unique_People_Spoke_To, na.rm=TRUE)+3, by = 3), include.lowest = TRUE, right = FALSE)) %>%
  count(bin)
hist_unique <- ggplot(hist_data, aes(x = bin, y = n)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#CC79A7", color = "black") +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), vjust = -0.2, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Number of Unique People Spoken To",
    subtitle = "Total unique conversational partners per participant (bin size = 3)",
    x = "Number of Unique People Spoken To (bin)",
    y = "Number of Participants"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) + margin_theme + angled_labels

# (B) Histogram for Expressiveness Score (bin size 0.2)
hist_data2 <- df %>%
  mutate(bin = cut(Expressiveness_Avg, breaks = seq(1, 5, by = 0.2), include.lowest = TRUE, right = FALSE)) %>%
  count(bin)
hist_express <- ggplot(hist_data2, aes(x = bin, y = n)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "#56B4E9", color = "black") +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), vjust = -0.2, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Expressiveness Scores",
    subtitle = "Participant self-reported expressiveness (score range: 1-5, bin size = 0.2)",
    x = "Expressiveness Score (bin)",
    y = "Number of Participants"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) + margin_theme + angled_labels

# (C) Scatterplot w/ regression line
scatter_plot <- ggplot(df, aes(x = Expressiveness_Avg, y = Unique_People_Spoke_To)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.65, color = "#2C3E50", size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#0072B2", size = 1.2) +
  labs(
    title = "Expressiveness vs Number of Unique People Spoken To",
    subtitle = "Linear regression with 95% confidence band",
    x = "Expressiveness Score (1 = Low, 5 = High)",
    y = "Number of Unique People Spoken To"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) + margin_theme

# 5. Show all plots interactively
print(hist_unique)
print(hist_express)
print(scatter_plot)

# 6. Save plots to file, unclipped and with wide aspect ratio for readability
ggsave("hist_unique.png", hist_unique, dpi = 320, width = 12, height = 7)
ggsave("hist_express.png", hist_express, dpi = 320, width = 12, height = 7)
ggsave("scatter_plot.png", scatter_plot, dpi = 320, width = 8, height = 6)

# 7. Statistical models and diagnostics
lm_mod <- lm(Unique_People_Spoke_To ~ Expressiveness_Avg + Age_Range + First_Language, data = df)
summary(lm_mod)
autoplot(lm_mod)
ggsave("lm_diagnostics.png", autoplot(lm_mod), dpi = 320, width = 10, height = 7)

po_mod <- glm(Unique_People_Spoke_To ~ Expressiveness_Avg + Age_Range + First_Language,
              family = poisson(link = "log"), data = df)
summary(po_mod)
overdispersion <- sum(residuals(po_mod, type = "pearson")^2) / po_mod$df.residual
print(sprintf("Overdispersion parameter: %.2f", overdispersion))
if(overdispersion > 2) {
  nb_mod <- glm.nb(Unique_People_Spoke_To ~ Expressiveness_Avg + Age_Range + First_Language, data = df)
  summary(nb_mod)
}


```
